texto: But when a man draws a lifeless thing into his passionate longing for dialogue, lending it independence and as it were a soul, then there may dawn in him the presentiment of a world-wide dialogue with the world-happening that steps up to him even in his environment, which consists partially of things. Or do you seriously think that the giving and taking of signs halts on the threshold of that business where an honest and open spirit is found?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Martin Buber que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “But when a man draws a lifeless thing into his passionate longing for dialogue, lending it indepe…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, being, buber, com foco em inspiracao e termino.

texto: Trust, trust in the world, because this human being exists - that is the most inward achievement of the relation in education. Because this human being exists, meaninglessness, however hard pressed you are by it, cannot be the real truth. Because this human being exists, in the darkness the light lies hidden, in fear salvation, and in the callousness of one's fellow-men the great Love.
explicacao: Citação de Martin Buber sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Trust, trust in the world, because this human being exists - that is the most inward achievement …” articula reflexao, education, faith em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e faith.
